title: Charles Samuels
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Charles Samuels
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: I joined the Christopher Newport faculty in August 2015. I finished my Ph.D. in 2007 under the supervision of Dr. Jeffrey D. Vaaler at the University of Texas at Austin and worked as a lecturer in the same department the following academic year. I spent one year as a postdoctoral fellow at the Max-Planck-Institut für Mathematik in Bonn, Germany followed by two years simultaneously at the University of British Columbia and Simon Fraser University in Vancouver, British Columbia.From 2011 to 2015 I was an Assistant and Associate Professor of Mathematics at Oklahoma City University.
education_entries: Ph D in Mathematics, University of Texas at Austin
research_interests: My current research lies at the intersection of Functional Analysis and Number Theory. In particular, I seek to categorize extensions of classical number theoretic functions, such as the prime Omega function, to the field of algebraic numbers.
teaching_interests: My teaching interests range from introductory level mathematics courses such at Precalculus and Calculus up through many 300 and 400 level courses for majors. I am particularly well suited to teaching Algebraic Structures, Real Analysis, Complex Analysis, Number Theory and Linear Algebra.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2026). A classification of Q-linear maps from the group of non-zero algebraic numbers modulo torsion to R . Canadian Mathematical Bulletin. Volume, 69. Issue, 2. Pages, 609-627.
Journal Article, Academic Journal The Correspondence Between Consistent Maps and Measures on the Places of Q-bar . New Zealand Journal of Mathematics. Pages, 14.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). Consistent maps and their associated dual representation theorems . Colloquium Mathematicum. Volume, 178. Issue, 1. Pages, 19.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). A Classification of Certain Rational Valued Linear Functionals . Acta Arithmetica. Volume, 205. Issue, 4. Pages, 29.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). A Polynomial Time Test to Detect Numbers with Many Exceptional Points . International Journal of Number Theory. Volume, 17. Issue, 4. Pages, 973-990.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Direct Limits of Adele Rings and Their Completions . Rocky Mountain Journal of Mathematics. Volume, 50. Issue, 3. Pages, 1021-1043.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2018). Metric Mahler Measures over Number Fields . Acta Mathematica Hungarica. Volume, 154. Issue, 1. Pages, 12.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). Counting Exceptional Points for Rational Numbers Associated to the Fibonacci Sequence . Periodica Mathematica Hungarica. Pages, 23.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). Continued Fraction Expansions in Connection with the Metric Mahler Measure . Monatshefte fuer Mathematik. Volume, 181. Issue, 4. Pages, 907-935.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Optimal Factorization of Rational Numbers Using Factorization Trees . International Journal of Number Theory. Volume, 11. Issue, 3. Pages, 739-769.

Grants and Sponsored Research: Charles Samuels, "AMS-Simons Research Enhancement Grant for Primarily Undergraduate Institution (PUI) Faculty.," Sponsored by American Mathematical Society and Simons Foundation
